title: WordPress REST Search Flow
activity_flow: ROUTE: register_routes() maps GET /search → get_items() with permission_callback=get_items_permission_check()
PERMISSION CHECK: get_items_permission_check($request) → always returns true (open to all)
PARAM SANITIZATION: sanitize_subtypes($subtypes, $request, $parameter) is called as sanitize_callback for 'subtype' param
└─ wp_parse_slug_list($subtypes) — normalize to array of slugs
└─ IF 'any' in subtypes → return ['any'] (overrides all other subtypes)
└─ IF handler is WP_Error → return WP_Error
└─ RETURN array_intersect($subtypes, $handler->get_subtypes()) — filter to valid subtypes
└─ IF result missing 'ids' or 'total' → return WP_Error('rest_search_handler_error', 500)
└─ array_map('absint', $result['ids']) — cast IDs to positive integers
└─ get_fields_for_response($request) — determine which fields to include
└─ $handler->prepare_item($id, $fields) — fetch item data (abstract, implemented by subclass)
└─ filter_response_by_context($data, $context) — strip fields not in context ('view'|'embed')
└─ $handler->prepare_item_links($id) — get HAL links (abstract, implemented by subclass)
END: Return paginated collection response with HAL links
key_conditions: Permission check always returns true — search is public
'any' subtype value overrides and collapses all other subtype filters
Type must match a registered search handler key or 400 error is returned
Handler result must contain both RESULT_IDS and RESULT_TOTAL or 500 error is returned
Requesting a page beyond max_pages (when results exist) returns 400 error
Context defaults to 'view'; response fields filtered accordingly

methods: get_item_schema(): array — builds JSON schema from handler types/subtypes
get_collection_params(): array — adds 'type' and 'subtype' params with sanitize_callback

error_paths: get_items: handler search_items returns malformed result → WP_Error('rest_search_handler_error', 500)
sanitize_subtypes: invalid subtype values → WP_Error from rest_parse_request_arg
important_notes: Handler registry is keyed by type string (e.g. 'post'). The controller delegates all actual DB searching to the handler via search_items(). prepare_item_for_response calls get_search_handler again — it is called twice per item in a loop. Pagination uses standard WP REST conventions: X-WP-Total, X-WP-TotalPages headers, and prev/next Link headers.
